business.headline: Government retains 4% retail inflation target till 2031The central government has retained the 4% retail inflation target with a 2–6% tolerance band for another five years, from April 2026 to March 2031, in consultation with the RBI.

Google, Meta found liable for $3 million in damages in online addiction suitThe lawsuit sought USD 3 million in damages. The outcome could influence thousands of similar cases against tech companies brought by parents, attorneys general and school districts.

In a 1st since 2019, India buys Iran LPG cargo as US eases sanctions, claims reportIndia had shunned energy purchases from Iran in 2019 under pressure from Western sanctions. The tanker was initially bound for China, according to LSEG data.

Free gas up to Rs 500, zero security fee: Govt pushes PNG shift for householdsThe government's focus remains on maintaining supply and reducing dependence on imports, while ensuring households have access to reliable and affordable energy.

8th Pay Commission timeline out: Here's what employees can expect nextIf you've been waiting for clarity on the 8th Pay Commission, there's finally some movement. The government has shared a timeline, giving employees a better idea of when changes in salaries and pensions could come.

Thinking of buying property with a credit card? Read this firstExperts say buying property abroad falls under capital account transactions, which come with strict rules. Credit cards are meant for daily expenses like travel or shopping, not for large investments like real estate.
Sensex ends 1,200 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; Titan up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1205.00 points to end at 75,273.45, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 394.05 points to close at 23,306.45.

Can mutual funds be gifted? All you need to know about Sebi's PPI planThe idea is simple. A person can purchase a Gift PPI using bank transfer or UPI. This prepaid card can then be gifted to another person. The recipient can redeem the card on an asset management company's platform and invest the amount in mutual funds.

Gold jumps Rs 6,000, silver rises Rs 14,000: Smart buying opportunity or risk?Gold and silver have made a strong comeback, drawing fresh attention from investors. But with prices rising so quickly, is this a good time to buy or a moment to pause and reassess?

41% borrowers are Gen Z: Is hidden debt rising among young Indians?A growing number of young Indians are turning to credit early in life. With Gen Z now making up 41% of borrowers, is this a sign of financial confidence or a warning of rising hidden debt? Let's find out.

LPG booking timelines unchanged; Centre assures steady supplyAuthorities have urged people not to believe or share unverified information. They have also advised against panic booking of cylinders, stressing that there is enough supply available across the country.

8th Pay Commission: Can government salaries finally match private sector perks?For years, government employees have measured their pay against private sector packages. Now, with the 8th Pay Commission seeking feedback, will it lead to a more balanced pay structure? Let's have a look.

Sensex jumps 1,300 points: Why is stock market rising today despite Iran war?Sensex, Nifty jump: The main trigger for today's rally is growing hope that tensions in the Iran conflict may ease. Reports suggest that the United States is working on a possible month-long ceasefire and has shared a 15-point plan with Iran.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets rise as crude oil prices fall?Early signals suggest that investors may return to equities after a volatile phase driven by war concerns and rising energy costs. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,095 as of 8:03 am, indicating that the Nifty may open above Tuesday's close of 22,912.40.
Sensex, Nifty gain over hopes of de-escalation in West Asia WarSensex jumps 700 points, Nifty above 23,100; SBI up 2%

West Asia war brings Indian aviation to its kneesA spike in global crude prices, closure of busy aviation hubs, and sweeping airspace restrictions across West Asia have collectively delivered a body blow to the airline industry that no carrier was prepared for.

Not just $580 million oil trades, huge S&P bets were placed just before Trump postWhat began with unusually timed oil trades worth around $580 million has now widened, with massive bets in S&P 500 futures also coming under scrutiny, even as the White House has dismissed the allegations.

Bangladesh hikes jet fuel prices by nearly 80% amid global energy crisisThe Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) on Tuesday increased jet fuel prices by nearly 80 percent, marking the second revision this month. The move comes as the ripple effect of rising global fuel rates, as tensions in key oil-producing regions continue to disrupt market stability.
Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil after US waiver, sources sayThe development comes after the US administration announced a 30-day waiver on certain sanctions related to Iranian oil.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has suggested that India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 7: Fastest Rs 1,000 crore grosser worldwide, eyes Kalki Dhurandhar 2 has joined Pushpa 2 as the fastest Indian Rs 1,000 crore grosser worldwide. The Aditya Dhar spy thriller is performing phenomenally, but it's the consistent single-day wins that are changing the game for Bollywood.

No songs like Shararat in Dhurandhar 2: Choreographer Vijay Ganguly explains why Choreographer Vijay Ganguly explains why Dhurandhar: The Revenge skipped a song like Shararat, and shares insights on Aditya Dhar's storytelling, Ranveer Singh's performance, and the film's impact.

Critics didn't understand my work, weren't qualified: Abhishek Bachchan Abhishek Bachchan reflects on his early scepticism towards critics and reveals how his perspective changed over time, shaping his career.

Nani's The Paradise shoot nears completion, director Sujeeth's Bloody Romeo next Nani likely to wrap The Paradise soon, plans to begin Bloody Romeo in June 2026 as reports suggest next move after ongoing shoot.

Mohanlal's Drishyam 2 to debut in theatres ahead of Drishyam 3's May release Drishyam 2 to re-release, this time in theatres on April 10 as makers build momentum for Drishyam 3, now set to release on May 21.

Amid Dhurandhar buzz, Imran Khan says won't play hairy angry men covered in blood Actor Imran Khan opened up in a Reddit Ask Me Anything about Bollywood's changing trends, his film choices, and why he is steering clear of hyper-violent roles amid Dhurandhar hype.

Dhurandhar 2: Arijit Singh's Phir Se brings Ranveer Singh to tears, shares post Ranveer Singh shared how Arijit Singh's song Phir Se from Dhurandhar: The Revenge moved him to tears, asking fans if they felt the same emotional impact. The song's video was released on Wednesday.

Pak politician Nabeel Gabol's old interview resurfaces amid Dhurandhar 2 success Ever since the release of Dhurandhar, Pakistani politician Nabeel Gabol had claimed that Jameel Jamali's character, played by Rakesh Bedi, was based on him. Here's how he reacted to his portrayal in the film.

Talwiinder, RJ Mahvash, Elli Evram in talks for The Traitors India 2: Sources India Today has learnt that The Traitors India is gearing up for a new season, with big names like Talwiinder, RJ Mahvash and Elli AvrRam in talks to participate. Karan Johar will return as host and producer.

Dhurandhar 2 unstoppable overseas with Rs 250 crore haul, never-seen-before buzz Dhurandhar 2 continues its global rampage, delivering record-breaking numbers across overseas markets. Here's how the Ranveer Singh starrer is rewriting box office history in just days.

Jaadu x Rocky: Jealous of Hrithik Roshan, Ryan Gosling pitches a wild crossover Hollywood star Ryan Gosling praised Hrithik Roshan and his iconic alien character Jaadu from Koi...Mil Gaya. During promotion of his upcoming film Project Hail Mary, he also hinted at a potential crossover.

Nivin Pauly reacts to comparisons with Mohanlal ahead of Prathichaya release Nivin Pauly has dismissed comparisons with Mohanlal, opening up about his laid-back and instinctive approach to acting ahead of the release of his upcoming film Prathichaya on March 26.

Inside pics from SS Rajamouli's grand Varanasi sets in Hyderabad Rajamouli recreated Varanasi in Hyderabad for Mahesh Babu-Priyanka Chopra's epic adventure film. Pictures from the sets show miniature sets of Varanasi based on which the extravagant sets are being built.

All about Harshil Kalia, 30-year-old actor-model who died in Jaipur crash The death of 30-year-old actor-model Harshil Kalia in a late-night car crash has shocked the entertainment industry. A rising face in regional cinema, her promising career was cut short.

BTS' 18.4 million-strong comeback: Why the record-breaking spree has just begun BTS The Comeback Live: ARIRANG drew in a staggering global viewership of 18.4 million on Netflix, surpassing the records of major awards shows across streaming, including that of the Grammys (14.4M) and VMAs (5.5M), and more.

He's in film, not in theatre: The auto driver who can't afford to watch Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge is smashing box office records, yet the auto-rickshaw driver who appeared briefly in the film hasn't been able to watch it in theatres due to financial constraints.

Dhurandhar 2's terrifying bathroom scene, and what Aditya Dhar told Pinda Actor Udaybir Sandhu, opened up on Aditya Dhar's feedback on his terrifying bathroom scene in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Udaybir portrayed Ranveer Singh's estranged friend Pinda in the espionage thriller.

Dhurandhar 2 effect: Canada theatre adds rare 15-minute-long interval, fans react Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has brought back the intermission in Canadian theatres after nearly 20 years, thanks to its long runtime and massive buzz. The film is dominating the domestic and global box offices.

The Rasputin story that changes how you see that one Madhavan scene in Dhurandhar 2 A closer look at how Aditya Dhar uses Rasputin in Dhurandhar: The Revenge to transform a viral scene into layered storytelling - from the folklore of Grigori Rasputin to the cinematic unravelling of power and betrayal.

general news.headline: Iran reviews US proposal but insists 'no talks' with WashingtonSpeaking to state television, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the proposal is under consideration by Iran's top leadership, even as he rejected any suggestion of direct engagement. Araghchi also confirmed that Washington has been sending messages through multiple intermediaries, but stressed that Tehran does not view this as formal dialogue.

Iran says it targeted US F-18 fighter jet, releases video; Pentagon rejects claimThe IRGC claimed it had "successfully targeted" the US aircraft, though it did not immediately provide details on the location, extent of damage or the fate of the pilot. There has been no immediate confirmation from the United States military, which has previously disputed similar claims by Iran during the conflict.

President Trump does not bluff, prepared to unleash hell: White House warns IranThe White House warns Iran to accept military defeat or face harsher US strikes, as Trump signals escalation even while ongoing talks continue amid rising tensions and uncertain diplomatic outcomes.

Live: Nearly 300 US soldiers have been injured since Iran war started, CENTCOM saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On the fourth week of the US-Israel-Iran war, Washington sent a 15-point truce plan to Tehran to end the Middle East conflict. The conditions include strict curbs on Tehran's nuclear programme, an end to support for proxy groups and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Washington is also pushing for a month-long ceasefire to enable negotiations. However, with Iran's military dismissing Trump's claim of negotiation talks, it seems unlikely that Iran will accept a proposal compromising on its nuclear facilities. 

Meanwhile, both Iran and Israel continue to attack each other on Wednesday. Israel targeted several places in Tehran with newly launched air raids on Wednesday. Iran, on the other hand, targeted US and Israeli military bases in the Gulf region in retaliatory measures. Catch all the live updates of the prevailing Middle East conflict on Indiatoday.in-

Pakistan's Khawaja Asif mocks US war goals amid deepening Hormuz crisisIn a pointed comment, Khawaja Asif indicated that the focus of war now seems to have shifted to reopening the Strait of Hormuz -- a route that was functioning before the conflict began, but was later shut by Iran after US and Israeli strikes on Tehran.

As India grapples with LPG crisis, a friend 20,000 kms away extends helping handThe disruption in maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has significantly impacted India's energy supply chain, forcing New Delhi to accelerate efforts to diversify sourcing.

Watch: Rishi Sunak disappointed his parents. He tells Nikhil Kamath the reasonThe episode, featuring Sunak and his wife Akshata Murty, marks the first time Kamath has done a "couple format", and the tone is markedly informal. Instead of policy or politics, the conversation moves through family expectations, relationships and the realities of public life.

India not a dalal nation, S Jaishankar tells all-party meet on Pak mediationThe External Affairs Minister's remarks came after Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said Islamabad was ready to host negotiations, even as the West Asia conflict entered its fourth week.

Will end war if conditions met, won't let Trump dictate: Iran rejects US proposalIran has rejected the US ceasefire proposal as "excessive," saying it will end the war only on its own terms and not allow Donald Trump to dictate the timeline.

Why India is pushing LPG users to switch to PNG | Explained in 5 pointsEnergy disruption due to the war in the Middle East has exposed India's dependence on imported gas. It has prompted a policy shift mandating a switch from LPG to piped natural gas (PNG) where available. With supply constraints looming, the government is fast-tracking pipelines and pushing a nationwide transition to strengthen energy security.

Gen Z spends on therapy, flowers, self-care. Will salaries need to catch up now?A Bengaluru expense post has gone viral for a reason. Gen Z is treating therapy and self-care as essentials, not extras. As priorities shift, the big question is no longer what young professionals spend, but whether salaries are built to keep up with this new reality.

Never ever dare: CJI Kant to man who called his brother over Supreme Court orderThe matter pertains to a petition filed by one Nikhil Kumar Punia, who was born into an upper-caste Hindu family but supposedly converted to Buddhism later. He, along with another General Category candidate, has sought minority reservation benefits for admission to a Buddhist medical college on grounds of their purported conversion.

UK court rejects Nirav Modi's plea to reopen extradition case in fresh setbackNirav Modi, declared a fugitive economic offender, is accused of defrauding PNB of over Rs 6,400 crore through fraudulent financial instruments.

Israel in race to bomb Iran targets amid fears Trump may abruptly end war: NYTIsrael is accelerating strikes on Iranian targets amid fears Trump could end the war, while Netanyahu says the broader campaign continues and Hezbollah remains a key military priority.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 7: Fastest Rs 1,000 crore grosser worldwide, eyes KalkiDhurandhar 2 has joined Pushpa 2 as the fastest Indian Rs 1,000 crore grosser worldwide. The Aditya Dhar spy thriller is performing phenomenally, but it's the consistent single-day wins that are changing the game for Bollywood.

Medical student found dead inside car, family claims abetment of suicide by doctorIn an official statement issued by her college, the institution has claimed that the woman was long under treatment for a mental health condition and that she had attempted suicide in the past.

RG Kar victim's mother to make poll debut as BJP fields her for Bengal electionsThe announcement came days after Ratna Debnath said she wanted to contest 2026 elections on BJP ticket, aiming to oust Trinamool Congress, alleging it obstructed justice for her daughter.
More rain soon: Western disturbances to hit India. See forecastJust as North India was preparing to begin summer, the weather is bound to shift again.

Caught on CCTV: Speeding car hits two-wheeler in Kerala; man, daughter injuredA father and daughter were injured in Coimbatore after a speeding car rammed into their scooter, as seen in CCTV footage. The impact flung them off the vehicle before the car hit another vehicle.

Google, Meta found liable for $3 million in damages in online addiction suitThe lawsuit sought USD 3 million in damages. The outcome could influence thousands of similar cases against tech companies brought by parents, attorneys general and school districts.

ED attaches Burj Khalifa flats, other high-end properties in Mahadev app caseThe properties, spread across Dubai and New Delhi, are valued at a total of approximately Rs 1,700 crore and are allegedly owned by persons and entities linked to Sourabh Chandrakar, one of the main promoters of the Mahadev betting app, per officials.

No songs like Shararat in Dhurandhar 2: Choreographer Vijay Ganguly explains whyChoreographer Vijay Ganguly explains why Dhurandhar: The Revenge skipped a song like Shararat, and shares insights on Aditya Dhar's storytelling, Ranveer Singh's performance, and the film's impact.

India's first patient recovery guidelines: All you need to knowA new set of national guidelines aims to reshape how doctors approach recovery – placing vitamins and minerals at the centre of the journey.

Didn't know he's a billionaire: Man who rescued Gautam Singhania after boat crash"At that time, we did not know that one of them was an Indian billionaire," a Maldives official said after rushing to aid victims of a speedboat crash involving industrialist Gautam Singhania.

sports.headline: Pakistan Super League 2026: Squads, schedule and all you need to know PSL 2026 begins on March 26 with defending champions Lahore Qalandars facing debutants Hyderabad Kingsmen at Gaddafi Stadium, as the expanded eight-team tournament runs until May 3 with 44 matches.

Loved young MS Dhoni more, he is India's greatest white-ball captain: Sourav Ganguly Former India captain Sourav Ganguly said he preferred the younger, fearless version of MS Dhoni, recalling how the wicketkeeper-batter from Jharkhand could effortlessly hit sixes under pressure. Dhoni made his international debut under Ganguly's captaincy in 2004.

Who doesn't want to play for India? Prithvi Shaw eyes strong season for DC IPL 2026: Prithvi Shaw eyes India comeback after move to Maharashtra and return to Delhi Capitals, aiming to rediscover form and consistency in IPL 2026 after a difficult phase in his career.

Impact Player to ball change: What was discussed at IPL captains' meet? BCCI officials and franchise captains met in Mumbai to finalise IPL 2026 playing conditions, addressing the contentious Impact Player rule, ball-change protocols for dew, and stricter match-day training guidelines.

Navdeep Saini replaces injured Harshit Rana for Kolkata Knight Riders IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders have roped in Navdeep Saini as a replacement for Harshi Rana, who has been ruled out of the tournament due to a igament strain in his right knee.

Will RCB bowling struggle without Hazlewood, Yash Dayal? Aakash Chopra reacts Aakash Chopra recently shared his views on RCB's bowling lineup, expressing concerns that the team could face challenges with Josh Hazlewood set to miss a few matches and Yash Dayal unavailable for the entire season.

From a challenge to a legacy: How Naveen John's unlikely cycling journey started A challenge with his college friend made Naveen John start a side quest to come back to India to become a national champion in cycling. The 39-year-old opens up on his journey in the sport, not chasing the Olympic dream as an athlete but setting it up for future talent.

Arshdeep Singh struggling in T20s? Irfan Pathan analyses India pacer's Achilles heel Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan has urged Arshdeep Singh to improve his new-ball bowling ahead of IPL 2026, pointing to a lack of inswing and a dip in effectiveness since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Ashwin's talking bull****: Yograj slams India legend for writing off Arjun Tendulkar IPL 2026: Yograj Singh fires back at Ashwin for writing off Arjun Tendulkar at LSG, saying he could make him surpass every batsman in the world within six months.

IPL 2026 preview: Injuries pile up, but do KKR have a Rahane problem too? IPL 2026: Three-time champions Kolkata Knight Riders will be aiming for a successful campaign after an underwhelming outing in 2025. But with injuries mounting and a slight combination issue, can Kolkata spread some joy to their fans?

Villain Gautam Gambhir doesn't worry about being liked by anyone: Ex-CSK star IPL 2026: Former CSK star Faf du Plessis and ex-India opener Aakash Chopra praised Gautam Gambhir's leadership and weighed in on whether Kolkata Knight Riders can thrive without their former mentor.

No stupid videos: Jason Gillespie makes his intent clear on Pakistan return in PSLJason Gillespie's return to Pakistan cricket ahead of PSL 2026 has created a buzz, with the former Pakistan red-ball coach going viral for bluntly refusing to film a "stupid" arrival video for Hyderabad Kingsmen.

Incredible Amelia Kerr joins Rohit Sharma in elite club with 2nd T20I centuryNZW vs SAW: Amelia Kerr continued her incredible run as captain after scoring a 52-ball century in the fifth and final T20I against South Africa at the Hagley Oval in Christchurch.

Injury or routine visit? Why Jasprit Bumrah is at BCCI COE before IPLIPL 2026: Fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah has not been part of the Mumbai Indians training camp. He checked into the BCCI Centre for Excellence in Bengaluru, but speculation about a fresh injury has now been debunked.

Gautam Gambhir gets personality rights protection in deepfake caseThe Delhi High Court has granted Gautam Gambhir protection of his personality rights and ordered the removal of objectionable deepfake and misleading content circulating across social media and internet platforms.

5th T20I: Esterhuizen, bowlers power South Africa to 3–2 series win vs New ZealandSouth Africa secured their first away T20I series under Shukri Conrad and a second successive bilateral series with a convincing performance against New Zealand in Christchurch.

Ben Duckett's withdrawal raises concerns over English players' future in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Ben Duckett's withdrawal from the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League has sparked fresh concerns over the reliability of English players in the league, with former India opener Aakash Chopra warning that franchises may rethink investing in young cricketers from England.

Dark chocolate for dark boy: L Sivaramakrishnan says he faced racism from teammatesLaxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed how teenage encounters with racism and systemic bias shattered his confidence, offering a haunting new perspective on why one of India's greatest bowling prodigies saw his career unravel.

Livingstone still up for England selection despite explosive interview: Rob KeyRob Key said that Liam Livingstone is still under consideration for England selection despite his public outburst against the board and the current team management. Livingstone has been out of the England setup since 2025.

How can Rishabh Pant find consistency as a T20 player? Faf du Plessis decodesFaf du Plessis has said that Rishabh Pant needs to be a high-impact player to find consistency as a T20 player. The LSG captain has impressed in Tests for India but hasn't found the same vein of form in white-ball cricket.

Real Madrid injury mismanagement: Mbappe played with torn knee after wrong scanReal Madrid have come under intense scrutiny after reports of a serious medical error involving Kylian Mbappe, who was allegedly allowed to play with a knee injury following a diagnostic mix-up.

Can a reimagined Rajasthan Royals get it right under Riyan Parag?IPL 2026 preview: Rajasthan Royals head into the new season amid buzz of an ownership change and with Riyan Parag as their new captain. But the big question remains: can they finally end their long wait for glory?

Virat Kohli has set a great example: Kaif urges Rahane to learn from RCB starIPL 2026: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif advised KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane to take inspiration from Virat Kohli's improved game against spin and convert his starts into match-winning knocks.

Rajasthan Royals: The $67 million IPL shadow that became a $1.63 billion empireStarting as one of the low-key sides in IPL, Rajasthan Royals finds itself as an empire now after its USD 1.63 billion sale. From winning the inaugural IPL title under Shane Warne to being filled with controversies, here's a look at the Jaipur-based franchise.

Griezmann joins ex-Barcelona teammate Messi in MLS, signs for Orlando CityFrench forward Antoine Griezmann is set to join MLS club Orlando City from Atletico Madrid in 2026. Griezmann will reunite with former Barcelona teammate and Inter Miami superstar Lionel Messi in the league.

IPL Impact Player rule to stay, says Sourav Ganguly after Axar Patel's criticismIPL 2026: All-rounders have openly expressed their disdain for it, but former India captain Sourav Ganguly on Tuesday said the Indian Premier League's controversial Impact Player rule is here to stay.

Most of ECB hierarchy should be sacked: Boycott slams England's Ashes reviewFormer England captain and legendary batter Geoffrey Boycott slammed ECB chief executive Richard Gould and managing director Rob Key over the review into England's Ashes defeat earlier in the summer.

A champion at last, Virat Kohli now walks into IPL 2026 without chainsIPL 2026: The wait is over, and so is the weight that followed Virat Kohli through every IPL season. Now, with nothing left to prove and only milestones ahead, IPL 2026 could unleash his most dangerous, unrestrained version yet.

Give us 0.5%: Netherlands cricketer's blunt take on Rs 16,660 crore RCB saleAfter RCB's Rs. 16,660 crore sale to a multi-industry consortium, Netherlands pacer Paul van Meekeren said that 0.5 percent of that price is enough to change Dutch cricket and help them bridge the gap to big teams in international cricket.

Salah to leave Liverpool at the end of the season, ends 9-year stint at AnfieldMohamed Salah will leave Liverpool at the end of the season, the club confirmed on Tuesday, bringing the curtain down on one of the most decorated spells in the club's history.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
